title: Ethiopian Fish Pilaf
description: A light and tender Ethiopian dish featuring guanciale and fish, with a bitter and flaky texture, made pescatarian-friendly with the use of fish instead of meat, and mantecare-emulsify method to create a creamy sauce.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the guanciale in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy.
Remove the guanciale from the skillet and set aside, leaving the grease in the skillet.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the berbere spice to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fish fillets to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until cooked through.
Add the fish stock, basmati rice, and butter to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the skillet.
Simmer the pilaf for 20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the chopped parsley, lemon juice, and crispy guanciale.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
